summary: Decompiled most of melee/gr/grlib.c to a reported 94.58% file match over 2.51 KB. The PR added a C implementation for grLib_801C9EE8 using entity collision data checks and updated grlib headers/includes, converted many grlib scratch entries from percentage scores to OK, and refreshed the grlib assembly listing/comments to real addresses and typed literals. There were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added grLib_801C9EE8(Vec3* point, float offset) logic in src/melee/gr/grlib.c, replacing a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub.

Introduced static inline inlineA0(Vec3* point, CollData* cd, float offset), which checks whether a point lies within an entity collision box expanded by offset using current ECB top/bottom/left/right and cur_topn coordinates.

grLib_801C9EE8 now iterates HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters, uses GET_FIGHTER, and tests Fighter.coll_data through inlineA0. The added source also contains an item-list path using HSD_GObj_Entities->items, itGetKind, GET_ITEM, Item.x378_itemColl, and a Pokemon_Random exclusion, though the exact control-flow intent is only partially evidenced by the diff.
Added includes needed by the new implementation: ft/inlines.h, ft/types.h, it/inlines.h, it/it_26B1.h, and math.h.

Updated grlib.h to include placeholder.h and changed the grLib_801C9EE8 prototype from no arguments to Vec3* plus float arguments; the header uses int return while the C definition shown uses bool, so the slice shows a type inconsistency or compatibility quirk rather than enough evidence to infer intent.
Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt entries for grLib functions around 801C96E8-801C9E60 from percentage statuses such as 100%, 99.91%, 83.46%, 90%, and 99.88% to OK.

Refreshed asm/melee/gr/grlib.s reference output: address comments changed from zero-based offsets to linked addresses like 0x801C96E8-0x801CA0B4, branch/call operands were resolved, local labels became address-based .L_801Cxxxx labels, strings such as "jobj.h" and "jobj" were expressed with .string, and sdata2 constants were typed as .float 0 and .float 0.5.
Removed an unnecessary include of baselib/fobj.h from src/sysdolphin/baselib/fog.h.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated point-versus-collision-box math into a static inline helper, making the grLib_801C9EE8 implementation easier to tune for matching while keeping the hot comparison sequence localized.
Used the existing entity access conventions instead of raw casts: HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters/items, GET_FIGHTER, GET_ITEM, Fighter_GObj, Item_GObj, Fighter, Item, and CollData fields.
Matched float math idioms visible in the assembly/report area by using ABS and explicit 0.5f half-extents for both Y and X bounds.
Updated scratch metadata to OK only for functions reported as matching or accepted in the PR context, preserving remaining nearby non-OK entries such as func_801CA0B4.
Cleaned include dependencies opportunistically by removing baselib/fobj.h from fog.h when it was no longer needed by that header.
decomp_lessons: For stage grlib work, collision checks against fighters/items may be expressed in terms of CollData ECB current-corrected extents plus cur_topn, rather than hand-coded offsets, when the relevant structs are available.
Small inline helpers can be useful for matching repeated geometric tests: compute center as (edge_a + edge_b) * 0.5f + topn - point, then compare ABS(delta) against half-size plus offset.
When replacing scratch-derived implementations with accepted C, update config/GALE01/scratches.txt from percentage scores to OK so future workers do not revisit solved attempts.

When regenerating asm references, prefer linked address comments and typed data directives (.string, .float) over raw .4byte/.byte encodings where the toolchain supports them; it improves searchability and reviewability without changing semantics.

Header prototypes matter for future callsites: this PR establishes that grLib_801C9EE8 takes a Vec3* and a float offset, not void, but the return spelling should be checked because the diff shows bool in C and int in the header.
naming_conventions: Existing grlib function names kept the address-based decomp convention: grLib_801C96E8, grLib_801C9EE8, etc.
The helper was named inlineA0, an address/temporary-style inline name rather than a semantic name, suggesting the PR prioritized matching over final documentation.
Local assembly labels were normalized from .L_00000xxx to linked-address labels such as .L_801C97BC.
Scratch entries kept func_801Cxxxx and done__func_801Cxxxx naming while changing statuses to OK.
Entity variables used project-style prefixes like cur_fighter, cur_item, fp, and ip.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x20) in grLib_801C9EE8, indicating stack-padding was required or attempted for codegen/layout matching.
Used ABS comparisons and explicit 0.5f constants to reproduce half-extent collision math.
Kept grLib_801C9EE8 only partially matched according to the report: 460 bytes, score 3,400/11,500, 70.43%, while many other grlib functions in the same file reported 100.00%.

The generated asm diff mostly reflects address/comment/directive normalization rather than obvious semantic assembly changes: real linked addresses, resolved branch displacements, address-based labels, .string directives, and .float constants.
The scratch cleanup marks multiple historical near-matches and duplicate attempts as OK, which prevents stale lower-percentage solutions from competing with the accepted C implementation.
follow_up_queries: Check current src/melee/gr/grlib.h and src/melee/gr/grlib.c for the final grLib_801C9EE8 return type, because this PR slice shows bool in C but int in the header.
Search later PRs for grLib_801C9EE8 to see whether the remaining 70.43% mismatch was fixed and whether inlineA0 was renamed semantically.
Search for other grlib collision helpers using xA4_ecbCurrCorrect and cur_topn to consolidate naming or struct understanding.
Inspect whether the item-loop path in grLib_801C9EE8 was intentional or later corrected, since the shown fighter loop condition makes the else branch look difficult to reach.
